action.reasoning: Observation: Training started successfully (data loaded, pos_weight computed, model created with 3.3M params), but evaluation crashed with 'ValueError: Input contains NaN' - model predictions contain NaN values. Analysis: The GINEConvManual or global_mean_pool_manual likely produces NaN for edge cases (empty graphs, single-node graphs, or numerical overflow in BatchNorm). Also, the model output might have Inf values that become NaN after sigmoid. Decision: Fix by (1) clamping model outputs to prevent Inf/NaN, (2) replacing NaN predictions with 0.5 in evaluation, (3) adding robustness to scatter operations, (4) ensure complete train.py is written (it was truncated).
